column1: FIRST_PLUNGE (shown only on someone's very first plunge)
6: "First time? It only gets worse from here."
9: "You don't know what you're getting into. Literally."
11: "Bookmark this moment. You'll romanticize it later."
12: "This is the easiest one. We promise."
14: "Cold plungers everywhere just gained a sibling."
19: "At this point it's a personality trait."
20: "Your streak is the only warm thing about you."
23: "Skipping a day? Not on the streak's watch."
24: "Your streak has its own LinkedIn now."
25: "Long streaks are built on short tempers."
26: "Your streak is older than some friendships."
28: "Other apps gave up on you. ColdStreak didn't."
29: "Your streak is judging you for hesitating."
35: "You could just go for a walk instead."
36: "No one is making you do this."
39: "The first 15 seconds are the worst. Probably."
43: "Welcome to the longest 60 seconds of your life."
45: "Your body is begging you to stop. Your ego said no."
47: "Your sympathetic nervous system is awake. Very awake."
48: "Fight or flight? You chose 'sit very still.'"
50: "The water is fine. Your nervous system disagrees."
51: "Breathe through your nose. Or just panic. Either way."
52: "Your face is doing things. Weird things."
54: "Your hair stood up to applaud you."
56: "First plunge is free. After that, it's $19.99."
57: "Hot tubs are for the weak. (We're kidding. Hot tubs are great.)"
61: "Plot twist: it doesn't get easier. You get colder."
62: "Adventure is just suffering with good lighting."
71: "Welcome to the worst spa day ever."
73: "Coffee is going to taste incredible after this."
75: "If your friends could see you now, they'd call 911."
81: "One minute in and suddenly breathing is a skill."
83: "The app believes in you. Your body does not."
85: "Don't check the timer. You'll be sad."
88: "Time moves slower in cold water. Einstein-ish."
89: "A minute here is a year anywhere else."
91: "Tell the cold your problems. It won't listen."
95: "Your dopamine receptors are about to throw a party."
100: "Your vagus nerve is having a moment."
110: "Your shrinkage is temporary. Your bragging rights are forever."
113: "Wim Hof would be proud. Your toes wouldn't."
116: "You're crushing it. Or being crushed. Same thing."
117: "Cold water is the world's loudest meditation."
118: "Meditation, but the floor is lava. Cold lava."
119: "Box breathing? More like ice cube breathing."
123: "Pee now or forever hold your peace."
127: "Your body is rerouting traffic. To survive."
133: "You paid money to feel like this."
137: "Your survival instincts have left the chat."
140: "This is no longer wellness. It's commitment issues."
141: "Your ancestors are nervously watching from the afterlife."
142: "The polar bears are calling to check on you."
144: "Somewhere, your cardiologist just woke up sweating."
146: "Your significant other is rehearsing the eulogy."
147: "Your hypothalamus filed a missing person report."
150: "Cold? You're past cold now. You're a state of mind."
151: "Your nervous system has accepted its fate."
152: "Your body has stopped trying to warm you. It's given up."
153: "Brown fat is now begging for backup."
155: "You and the water are negotiating peace terms."
161: "Reality is melting. Wait, no, that's just you."
163: "Your spirit guide is a polar bear named Stan."
167: "You've earned the right to be insufferable about this."
168: "Your discomfort tolerance just reached PhD level."
170: "Goggins would offer you a fist bump. From a heated room."
171: "Navy SEALs are forwarding your video to each other."
172: "The Vikings would be impressed. The Vikings."
174: "Frostbite is now within rights to introduce itself."
177: "Your fingers have started a new life elsewhere."
178: "Your insurance premiums are about to rise."
181: "Your watch is asking if you're okay."
182: "Apple Health is now a hostage negotiator."
183: "Your future self is begging you to get out."
186: "Three personalities are now in this tub."
187: "You're discovering things about yourself. Concerning things."
188: "What started as wellness has become rebellion."
192: "6 minutes? Your ancestors are finally impressed."
193: "You've officially stayed in longer than most people stay motivated."
194: "At 6 minutes, this becomes a psychological experiment."
195: "6 minutes in freezing water. Therapy might've been easier."
197: "You're either building discipline or developing a supervillain origin story."
198: "Most people quit at discomfort. You scheduled it."
200: "6 minutes. The polar bears have accepted you."
203: "This app legally considers you amphibious now."
204: "At this point the cold is probably scared of you."
205: "You stayed in long enough to question every life decision."
206: "6 minutes? That's not recovery. That's character development."
207: "Doctors hate him. Fingers hate him more."
208: "You could've watched half a TikTok instead."
211: "This is no longer impressive. It's a cry for help."
212: "Your therapist would like to schedule an emergency session."
213: "You're in an Olympic event now. Solo. Underwater."
215: "Your loved ones are organizing an intervention."
216: "You've passed 'biohacker'. You're now in 'cult leader' territory."
219: "You're not building character. You're building a legend."
221: "The water has aged. You have aged. Civilizations have risen and fallen."
222: "Your phone has died of secondhand cold."
226: "You are the cold now. Pass it on."
230: "This is technically a medical emergency in 47 countries."
231: "Your insurance company just blocked your number."
232: "You're an outlier on every chart now."
234: "Wim Hof just texted: 'okay, take it down a notch.'"
236: "Your guardian angel has filed a workers' comp claim."
237: "The Reaper is in the lobby, but you're not on the list."
240: "Your name will be whispered in saunas."
242: "Children will learn of you in school."
244: "This is no longer voluntary. The cold won't let you leave."
245: "You and the tub are in a committed relationship now."
246: "The tub has called you 'home' three times now."
250: "This is no longer wellness. This is Arctic cosplay."
252: "The fish here have evolved beyond emotion."
253: "Even the polar bears would tap out."
254: "Antarctica is offended you call this cold."
256: "Ice cubes are looking at this water in horror."
258: "This is below the freezing point of self-respect."
264: "Polar bears use this water for bragging rights."
266: "The pain has its own zip code."
267: "There is no Mr. Heater that can save you."
272: "Your cells are passing notes about you."
276: "Your ancestors survived winters. You panic at 54°."
278: "This is basically a swimming pool. Toughen up."
279: "Your great-grandfather bathed in this. For fun."
280: "Real cold plungers would call this 'a refreshing dip.'"
282: "The Vikings would laugh at this temperature."
283: "Crank it down. You can handle more."
284: "This is what hot tubs feel like in Greenland."
287: "Are you sure this counts as a plunge?"
289: "Save the screenshots until it's actually cold."
291: "Don't tell the hardcore plungers about this."
GENERAL (always in the mix as fallback)
295: "Character development is stored in the cold."
296: "Imagine explaining this hobby to your grandparents."
299: "Future you keeps signing up for this."
300: "Congratulations, your survival instincts are functioning normally."
302: "This is what dopamine addiction looks like."
304: "The Vikings would still think this is weird."
307: "You are the bear. The bear is you."
315: "Normal people are eating pancakes right now."
317: "You will tell everyone about this. They will not care."
320: "Add this to your resume. They'll have questions."
324: "Aliveness has a price. The price is shivering."
325: "You're going to do this again tomorrow."
328: "Soon you'll be doing this in the ocean."
332: "You can do hard things. This is one of them."
334: "Bad day at work? Cold prepared you."
344: "You're winning. We can't prove it, but vibes."
347: "Your stress hormones are getting a workout."
348: "You will sleep like a stone tonight."
350: "Your aches and pains are also confused."
353: "Cold doesn't build character. It reveals it."
355: "Your ego doesn't fit in this tub."
356: "Trust the process. The cold, cold process."
362: "Discomfort is the teacher. The cold is the classroom."
364: "Pop quiz: how long can you stay?"
366: "Cold plungers don't die. They just freeze in time."
367: "The cold doesn't care about your podcast."
372: "Adventure is just suffering with good lighting."
374: "Resilience: now in a tub near you."
